ask: Users should be able to save jobs and get notified when similar ones appear.

story.title: Save Jobs + Notify on Similar Postings – Email Notification on Similar Job Detection
story.story: As a job seeker with saved postings, I want to receive an email notification when a similar job is detected, so that I can discover relevant new postings without manually monitoring the board.

acs.name: Notification Preference Opt-In at Save Time
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker is viewing a job posting they have not yet saved
acs.when: the job seeker saves the posting via the save action
thens: The posting will be added to the job seeker's saved list.
A notification preference toggle will be presented, defaulting to opted-in for similar-job email alerts for that saved posting.
The job seeker's notification preference state will be persisted in the `saved_jobs` table or related schema.

acs.name: Notification Preference Opt-In via History Page
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker is viewing the History page with at least one saved posting that has notifications disabled
acs.when: the job seeker enables the notification toggle for a specific saved posting
thens: The notification preference for that saved posting will be updated to opted-in.
The updated preference will be persisted immediately in the `saved_jobs` table or related schema.
No notifications will be sent retroactively for similar jobs detected before the opt-in was enabled.

acs.name: Notification Preference Opt-Out via History Page
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker is viewing the History page with at least one saved posting that has notifications enabled
acs.when: the job seeker disables the notification toggle for a specific saved posting
thens: The notification preference for that saved posting will be updated to opted-out.
The updated preference will be persisted immediately in the `saved_jobs` table or related schema.
No further similar-job email notifications will be sent for that saved posting.

acs.name: Similar Job Detected — Email Notification Sent
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker has a saved posting with notifications opted-in and the 24-hour batch pipeline has completed a similarity match
acs.when: the batch pipeline identifies a new posting as similar to the job seeker's saved posting
thens: An email notification will be sent to the job seeker's registered email address via Resend.
The email will identify the similar posting by title and include a link to view it.
The email will reference the specific similarity signal in calm, evidence-based language (e.g., "This job appears frequently across boards, like the one you saved").
The email will not use urgency language, marketing language, or present the similarity as a certainty.
The email will comply with all GUARDRAILS.md requirements for tone, evidence panels, and sensitivity context.

acs.name: No Notification Sent When User Is Opted Out
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker has a saved posting with notifications opted-out
acs.when: the batch pipeline identifies a new posting as similar to that saved posting
thens: No email notification will be sent to the job seeker for that similarity match.
The similarity match event will be recorded internally without triggering any outbound communication.

acs.name: Similar Job Already Saved by User — Notification Still Sent
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker has a saved posting with notifications opted-in and the similar posting detected by the batch pipeline is already in the job seeker's saved list
acs.when: the batch pipeline identifies the already-saved posting as similar
thens: An email notification will be sent to the job seeker for the similar posting.
The email will not suppress or alter its content based on the similar posting already being saved.

acs.name: Similar Job Detected in Same Batch Run as Save
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker saves a posting with notifications opted-in and a similar posting exists within the same 24-hour batch run
acs.when: the batch pipeline completes its similarity matching for that run
thens: An email notification will be sent to the job seeker for the similar posting detected in the same batch run.
Both the saved posting and the similar posting will appear independently in the job seeker's saved list if the similar posting was also saved.

acs.name: Re-Enabling Notifications Does Not Replay Missed Notifications
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker previously disabled notifications for a saved posting and then re-enables them via the History page
acs.when: the notification preference is updated to opted-in
thens: No email notifications will be sent for similar jobs that were detected during the period when notifications were disabled.
Only similar jobs detected after the re-enable timestamp will trigger email notifications.

acs.name: Referenced Job Posting No Longer Exists at Notification Time
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker has a saved posting with notifications opted-in and the original saved posting has been deleted or expired before the batch pipeline runs
acs.when: the batch pipeline identifies a similar posting and attempts to send an email notification
thens: The email notification will be sent without referencing unavailable details of the expired or deleted posting.
The email copy will handle the missing posting gracefully and will not display broken links or empty fields for the original saved job.
The email will still identify the similar posting by title and include a link to view it.

acs.name: Email Delivery Failure via Resend
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker has a saved posting with notifications opted-in and a similar job has been detected by the batch pipeline
acs.when: the Resend API returns an error during email delivery
thens: The notification will not be delivered to the job seeker.
No retry will be attempted for the failed notification.
The delivery failure event will be logged internally for observability.

acs.name: Duplicate Save of Same Posting — Independent Notifications
acs.given: a logged-in job seeker has saved the same job posting more than once (e.g., via different boards) with notifications opted-in on each saved entry
acs.when: the batch pipeline identifies a similar posting for that job
thens: Each saved entry for the same posting will be treated independently for notification purposes.

The behavior for whether duplicate saves trigger one or multiple notifications will follow the definition confirmed in [A8 — behavior for duplicate saves is undefined and must be confirmed by the team].

assumptions_carried: A1 — "Similar job" is defined as a posting that shares high repost frequency, similar age, or cross-board spread with a saved job. No explicit similarity threshold or weighting is provided; ghost-score signals are repurposed as-is.
A2 — Notification preference is a binary opt-in/opt-out per saved job (not global). No granular controls (e.g., "notify only for high-confidence matches") are in scope.
A3 — Email is the only notification channel; no SMS, in-app alerts, or push notifications are required.
A4 — Resend integration and event pipeline do not yet exist and must be built as a dependency; no timeline or ownership is specified.
A5 — "Similar" matching runs as part of the existing 24-hour batch ingest pipeline; no new scheduling or orchestration is required.
A6 — Users can opt in to notifications at the moment of saving a job or later via the History page; no separate settings page is assumed.

A7 — Notification copy must reference the similarity signal (e.g., "This job appears frequently across boards, like the one you saved") to comply with GUARDRAILS.md; no marketing or urgency language is permitted.
A8 — Duplicate saves of the same posting are not deduplicated in the UI; each save is treated independently for notification purposes.

warnings: ACE3: WHEN contains 'and' — likely two ACs.

researcher_brief:
# CONTEXT BRIEF

• **Saved jobs infrastructure exists but is minimal.** Users can already save postings (stored in `saved_jobs` table with `posting_id` and `saved_at`), and the History page displays them. However, saving is binary today with no status or metadata fields to track notification preferences or similarity matching. ([C1])

• **Notification infrastructure does not yet exist in AutoApply.** No notification system is currently wired into the product. Resend is the assumed email provider
analyst_spec:
# SCOPED SPEC: Save Jobs + Notify on Similar Postings

## IN SCOPE
- Allow users to save a job posting (already exists; no change required)
- Detect when a new job posting is "similar" to a user's saved job(s) using available signals (repost frequency, posting age, cross-board spread from ghost-score classifier)
- Trigger an email notification to the user when a similar job is detected
- Notification must comply with GUARDRAILS.md (calm tone, evidence-based framing, no false certainty)
- Store n

clarity.reasoning: The persona is clear ('job seeker with saved postings'), the intent is unambiguous, and language is plain. However, the title is compound ('Save Jobs + Notify on Similar Postings') suggesting two concerns, and the story itself is slightly verbose. Minor gap compared to the clean single-action reference.

testability.reasoning: Every AC uses proper GIVEN/WHEN/THEN structure with observable, future-tense outcomes. Edge cases are thoroughly covered (opt-out, delivery failure, expired postings, duplicate saves, re-enabling notifications). Exceeds the reference in edge case coverage.

scope_fidelity.reasoning: The generated story invents significant scope not present in the original ask or reference: per-posting notification preference toggles (AC1, AC2, AC3), opt-in/opt-out mechanics, History page UI changes, and Resend API specifics. The reference explicitly scopes to alert capability only, assuming saving already exists. The generated story conflates saving and alerting into a compound story and adds substantial UI/preference management scope.

completeness.reasoning: Assumptions are well-flagged with A1-A8 prefixes, dependencies are surfaced (Resend, batch pipeline), and edge cases are extensive. The reference's key assumptions (saving pre-exists, similarity algorithm is a spike, daily cadence) are partially addressed but the generated story treats saving as in-scope rather than pre-existing, which is a completeness gap relative to the reference framing.

grounding.reasoning: Claims are tied to context with explicit assumption flags. GUARDRAILS.md is referenced, Resend is named as a dependency, and the 24-hour batch pipeline is grounded. A8 is appropriately flagged as undefined. However, some specifics (e.g., 'ghost-score signals', 'saved_jobs table schema') appear as PLACEHOLDER-level assumptions that aren't fully flagged as unverified, and the GUARDRAILS.md reference assumes context not provided in the original ask.
